fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: You prefer passive index funds, so this tracks the Nifty Midcap 150 index with a low yearly fee of 0.2%. It gives exposure to fast-growing mid-sized companies, which can help beat inflation over 15 years.

fund.name: Nippon India Nifty Smallcap 250 Index Fund - Direct Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: This low-cost index fund (0.2% yearly fee) tracks small-sized Indian companies. These are riskier but have higher growth potential over your 15-year horizon, helping preserve wealth against inflation.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential NASDAQ 100 Index Fund - Direct Plan - Growth
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: This fund gives exposure to top US tech giants like Apple and Microsoft. It diversifies your portfolio globally, reducing risk from only holding Indian stocks. Low yearly fee of 0.2%.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large and Midcap Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.sub_category: Equity Scheme - Large & Mid Cap Fund
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: This actively managed fund picks both large and mid-sized Indian companies. It has delivered strong returns over 3 years and adds growth potential beyond pure index funds.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Gold ETF
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Gold acts as a safety net when stock markets fall. This low-cost ETF (0.4% yearly fee) tracks physical gold prices and helps protect your wealth during tough times.

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww, Kuvera, or Coin by Zerodha.
Step 2: Complete your KYC if not already done.
Step 3: Start monthly SIPs of ₹4,800, ₹3,200, ₹2,400, ₹3,200, and ₹2,400 in the five funds listed above.
Step 4: Choose the 'Direct Plan' and 'Growth Option' for each fund during setup.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Confirm all SIPs are running smoothly.
After 1 year: Check if any fund has underperformed its category average for 3 years.
Every 3 years: Review your risk comfort as you near retirement.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check your portfolio mix. If any category (like midcap or gold) has grown to more than 10 percentage points above its target (e.g., gold jumps from 15% to 26%), sell some and buy underweight categories to get back to your plan.
plan.rationale: At 53, with 15 years to go, you can afford to take aggressive risk for higher growth. Your ₹16,000/month SIP and ₹11.8L corpus will grow into a strong retirement nest egg. We've used low-cost index funds as you prefer, added US tech for global diversification, and included gold for safety. This mix aims to beat inflation and help you reach your ₹1 crore goal.
risks: Stock markets can drop 30% in a bad year — your portfolio value will fall temporarily but usually recovers over time.
Mid and small-cap funds are more volatile than large companies and may fall harder during market crashes.
Gold prices can stay flat for years and don't pay dividends.
Global funds (like NASDAQ) are affected by the US economy and currency changes.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

aps.reasoning:
The plan is predominantly passive in the equity sleeve, with 70% allocated to Nifty Midcap 150, Nifty Smallcap 250, and NASDAQ 100 index funds. The remaining 30% of equity is in an actively managed Large & Midcap fund, reducing the pure passive equity score from 1.0 to 0.7. However, non-equity allocations (Gold ETF) are not counted against this score per the instructions.

Turnover is very low (0.85) due to a simple 'once a year' rebalancing guideline with no tactical shifts. Cost emphasis is high (0.9) as the plan explicitly selects low-cost direct plans and index funds.

Research vs cost is heavily skewed toward broad market exposure (0.95); the plan avoids individual stock picking, relying entirely on index tracking or active fund mandates without requiring manual deep research.

Time horizon alignment (0.9) is excellent for a 15-year retirement goal, with static SIPs and long-term inflation-beating rationale.

Portfolio activeness (0.4) reflects a mixed picture: while the index components are truly passive, the 20% allocation to the Motilal Oswal Large & Midcap Fund is genuinely active (high tracking error of 13.05%, positive alpha of 18.62, beta 1.11). This active sleeve prevents the score from being a perfect 1.0.

pqs.reasoning: Plan quality is high. Goals (medical, inflation, travel) and horizon (15 yrs) align well with an aggressive 100% equity-heavy (90% equity, 10% gold) portfolio. Diversification is excellent across asset sizes (mega/mid/small), geography (India/US), and uncorrelated asset (gold). Tax efficiency is strong for a 'new_regime' investor because ELSS is not used (80C irrelevant) and the focus is on index funds/ETFs which avoids active debt funds that would be taxed at slab rates, even though gold has no tax benefit, it's a small portion. Consistency is high: low-cost passive selection matches the investor's preference, risk warnings match the aggressive nature, and the 15-year horizon justifies the volatility.
